theory.intro: Rounding replaces a number with the nearest 'round' number — a multiple of $10$, $100$, $1000$ and so on.
facts: Look at the digit **just to the right** of the rounding place.
If it is $5$ or more, round **up**; if it is $4$ or less, round **down**.
Every digit after the rounding place becomes $0$.
steps: Underline the digit in the place you are rounding to.
Check the next digit on its right.
Round up or stay, then fill the rest with zeros.
